SmilesDrawer
Home Playground Getting Started Examples
Home Playground Getting Started Examples

Getting Started

Three ways to start drawing molecules.

1

Just add a script tag

The fastest path. No build tools, no npm, no framework. Perfect for quick prototypes, Jupyter notebooks, or static HTML pages.

index.html

SmiDrawer.apply() scans the page for any element with a data-smiles attribute and renders the molecule into it. Works with both <svg> and <canvas> elements.

2

npm install

For bundled applications. Import as an ES module. 

npm install smiles-drawer
app.js 
import SmilesDrawer from 'smiles-drawer';

// Option 1: Quick apply (scans DOM for data-smiles attributes)
SmilesDrawer.SmiDrawer.apply();

// Option 2: Programmatic drawing
const drawer = new SmilesDrawer.SvgDrawer({ width: 500, height: 500 });
const svgElement = document.getElementById('my-molecule');

SmilesDrawer.parse('c1ccccc1', function(tree) {
    drawer.draw(tree, svgElement, 'light');
}, function(err) {
    console.error('Parse error:', err);
});
3

Framework examples

React

MoleculeViewer.jsx 
import { useRef, useEffect } from 'react';
import SmilesDrawer from 'smiles-drawer';

export default function MoleculeViewer({ smiles, theme = 'light' }) {
    const svgRef = useRef(null);

    useEffect(() => {
        if (!svgRef.current || !smiles) return;
        const drawer = new SmilesDrawer.SvgDrawer({ width: 400, height: 400 });
        SmilesDrawer.parse(smiles, (tree) => {
            svgRef.current.innerHTML = '';
            drawer.draw(tree, svgRef.current, theme);
        }, (err) => console.error(err));
    }, [smiles, theme]);

    return ;
}

Vue

MoleculeViewer.vue

Svelte

MoleculeViewer.svelte

HTML data-attribute pattern

The simplest approach for static content. Just set data-smiles on any SVG or canvas element and call apply().

Options Reference

Option Type Default Description
width Number 500 Width of the drawing canvas
height Number 500 Height of the drawing canvas
bondLength Number 15 Length of bonds in pixels
bondThickness Number 1.0 Thickness of bonds
bondSpacing Number 0.18 Spacing between double/triple bonds (fraction of bond length)
shortBondLength Number 0.85 Length of short bonds (fraction of bond length)
isomeric Boolean true Display stereochemistry (wedge/dash bonds)
debug Boolean false Show vertex indices and ring info
terminalCarbons Boolean false Show terminal carbon labels (CH3, etc.)
explicitHydrogens Boolean false Show explicit hydrogen atoms
compactDrawing Boolean true Use compact layout for macrocycles
padding Number 20.0 Padding around the molecule drawing
fontSizeLarge Number 6 Font size for atom labels
fontSizeSmall Number 4 Font size for subscripts (e.g., H count)
overlapSensitivity Number 0.42 Sensitivity for overlap detection
overlapResolutionIterations Number 1 Number of overlap resolution iterations
experimental Boolean false Enable experimental features
themes Object {...} Custom color themes (light/dark)
